title: All You Zombies
blog: Then I glanced at the ring on my finger. The Snake That Eats Its Own Tail, Forever and Ever. I know where I came from–but where did all you zombies come from? I felt a headache coming on, but a headache powder is one thing I do not take. I did once–and you all went away. So I crawled into bed and whistled out the light. You aren't really there at all. There isn't anybody but me–Jane– here alone in the dark. I miss you dreadfully! -- All You Zombies , Robert A. Heinlein

title: The Mind of God
blog: Is it possible to imagine anything so ridiculous as this miserable and wretched creature, which is not so much as master of himself, exposed and subject to offenses of all things; and yet dareth call himself master and emperor of this universe in whose power it is not to know the least part of it, much less to command the same? And the privilege, which he so fondly challengeth, to be the only absolute creature in this huge world's frame perfectly able to know the absolute beauty and several parts thereof, and that he is only of power to yield the great Architect thereof due thanks for it, and keep account both of the receipts and layings-out of the world! Who hath sealed him this patent? Let him show us his letters of privilege for so noble and so great a charge. ---Michael de Montaigne, An Apology of Raymond Sebond , 1568

title: Dear Higgs Boson
blog: It seemed only fair .
ocr: Dear Higgs Boson, We know you're out there. We can feel you now. We know that you're afraid. You're afraid of us; you're afraid of change. We don't know the future. We didn't write this to tell you how this is going to end. We wrote this to tell you how it's going to begin. As you know, our Large Hadron Collider has had some setbacks due to a.... uh... “transformer malfunction" but we know it was you. You sabotaged our machine. We hope you've been enjoying your vacation because we're scheduled to restart in September 2009 and we're pissed. ...S0 run and hide, asshole. Run and hide. If you should get careless and allow yourself to get detected by the Tevatron, we are going to be supremely disap- pointed; because we want to find you first, and when we do, rest assured we are not going to publish right away. We're going to teach you some manners first. Love, Pe CERN

title: The Simply Connected Underwear Theorem

title: The String Theory Landscape
ocr: OR, A VISION IN A DREAM A FRAGMENT In Academia did theoretical physicists A stately string theory decree: Where Branes, the sacred rivers, ran Through compactified dimensions measureless to man Down to a stable vacuum. So twice 5 x 10499 configurations of fertile ground With walls and towers were girdled round: And there were minima bright with sinuous rills, Where blossomed many a solution-bearing tree; And here were Calabi-Yau manifolds ancient as the hills, Enfolding sunny spots of positive vacuum energy. But oh! That deep romantic chasm which slanted Down the [Greene] hill of refutability! A savage place! as holy and enchanted As e’er beneath experimental result was haunted By the specter of falsifiability! And from this chasm, with ceaseless turmoil seething, As if theoretical physics in fast thick pants were breathing, A mighty fountain momently was forced: Amid whose swift half-intermitted burst... And thesis advisors everywhere did live in mortal fear, Since angry grad students by the hundreds did throng: As mathematical evidence came e'er so clear, That string theory indeed is wrong. THERE HE 5 YEARS OF 1S. GET HIM? GRAD SCHOOL , DOWN THE DRAIN!

On the other hand, it could just be an amazing coincidence.

title: Diary of a Dumb Jock Kid
blog: My apologies to Jeff Kinney. I actually think his book , is awesome, but it's clearly biased against dumb jocks.
ocr: Dear Diary, Today was just another typical day at school. Let me just say for the record that middle school is the dumbest idea ever invented. You got gorillas like me who need to shave twice a day mixed in with little pipsqueaks whose testicles haven't dropped yet. OUTTA MY WAY, RUNTS ! Before homeroom, I snuck into the janitor's closet with Kelly Farkas and Jen Fallon where we played a cool game called Seven Minutes in Heaven. ea JANITOR Sure, I'm only in middle school, but I stil get alotta nookie for my age. I guess it comes with the territory - me being captain of the wrestling team and all. God, I love being a jock! During third period, we had a math test sol made sure to sit behind this Korean kid. I heard that those people are good at math cine fr and stuff. T still don't understand fractions but I'm getting an "A" in math class. During lunch period, I ran into this loser kid named Greg or something. I can't stand him cuz he's always hanging out with this dorky fat kid like they wuz in love or something. Greg ) fat kid is So I beat the holy living shit outta him and took his lunch money for good measure. 3 » Anyway, I used the lunch money to buy a six-pack and I got drunk in the woods behind the school with a bunch of high school kids. Allin all, it was a pretty good day at school. I'd like to see this book written from the jock's point of view.

title: A Mini Tutorial
blog: As per the usual custom, it's your job to spot the errors in the comic.
ocr: The phenomenon of magnetism was first observed at least 2500 years ago in small fragments of magnetized iron ore. The earliest known surviving descriptions of magnets show that such phenomena were known to the people of ancient Greece, India, China, and parts of Asia Minor. These strange stones exhibited an odd polarity with a distinctive south pole and a north pole. Itwas observed that unlike poles of different magnets exerted an attractive force on each other while like poles repelled each other. It was also observed that when magnets were placed near certain other objects that previously exhibited no magnetic properties, the second object would also become magnetized. Today we know that a magnet produces an invisible magnetic field in the space around it and a second object responds to that field. This magnetic field can he visualized as invisible magnetic field lines emanating from the north pole and leading to the south pole. AE However, it should be kept in mind that the direction of the field lines is just a convention and does not imply that the magnetic field is actually moving. ‘Magnets always have a north and south pole. In other words, you can't break a magnet in half to obtain a magnet with just a north pole and a magnet with just a south pole. The two smaller pieces would, in fact, become separate magnets, each with its own north and south poles. E> Similarly, if you join the north pole of one magnet with the south pole of another magnet, the two magnets would act as a single magnet with a single north pole and a single south pole. Ey EY > E44 A magnet's magnetic field can be described by a vector quantity called the magnetic (dipole) moment. The strength of the magnetic field at any given point is proportional to the magnitude of the magnetic moment. When a magnet is placed in a magnetic field (from another source), it experiences a torque which tends to align the magnetic moment along the lines of the field. In the case of a bar magnet, you can visualize the magnetic moment by imagining the vector pointing from its south pole to its north pole. $wWN ‘Magnetic fields are created by moving electric charge (such as a moving electron or proton). For example, when a current moves through a wire, it produces a magnetic field around the wire as illustrated below. .. If you wind the wire into a coil as illustrated below, the magnetic field will reinforce itself. cURREWT. As you can see, a coil with a current running through it hecomes sort of like a bar magnet (called a solenoid) with a north and south pole. The more times you wind the coil, the stronger the magnetic field produced. You can make the magnet even stronger by winding the coil around a core of magnetic material (Way, congratulations. You just built a kind of magnet called an electromagnet). Every electron has its own magnetic moment due to an intrinsic property of the electron called spin. Spin is a quantum mechanical property of the electron but you can think of it as being similar to a classically spinning sphere. a ? Electrons not only spin; they also revolve around the nuclei of atoms. It was once thought that electrons orbited around the nucleus the same way that planets orbit around the sun but today we know that the picture is hit more complex (involving concepts from quantum mechanics that won't concern us here). Electrons occupy certain shells that surround the nucleus (which are numbered 1, 2, 3, ..). Each shell contains one or more subshells (which are given letter designations such as s, p, d, f) and each subshell contains one or more orbitals (an s subshell contains 1 orhital, a p subshell contains 3, ad subshell contains 5, ete). UNPaIRED ELectRon Only two electrons can occupy each orbital and the electron pair must have opposite spins (.e. if one electron in the pair has an up spin, then the other must have a down spin. duh). Hence, the magnetic fields of the electron pair within an orbital tend to cancel each other out. In addition to the magnetic field due to the electron's spin, somtimes there is an additional magnetic field produced by the movement of electrons within the orbitals. The atoms of certain elements have unpaired electrons in their outer orbitals which can cause the atom to have an overall net magnetic field. In some materials, these atomic magnetic moments tend to line up parallel to each other into regions called domains. Within each domain, the atomic magnetic moments are almost all aligned. Such materials are called ferromagnetic. Under normal conditions (.e. no externally applied magnetic field) the magnetization of the domains are aligned randomly and, hence, the material has no net magnetic field. However, when an external magnetic field is applied, the domains tend to align themselves parallel to the field, thus magnetizing the material. Wes [=< Rx] | ES Maeweric FIELD To make along story short, you can control the strength of the externally applied field such that the material retains a residual magnetization. The result is a permanent magnet like the kind you use to stick your kids' drawings to the refrigerator. thdr FUCKING MAGNETS How do they work? moving electric charge
image_title: I'm gonna have to side with Insane Clown Posse on this one.  Magnets ARE a miracle.  Magic everywhere in this bitch.

The electrical signal is amplified and filtered. Then the important information is Separated out ofthe signl and tls colt diaphragm — The signal is sent to a coil affixed toa — magnet. The electrical signal produces a ~~ varying magnetic field in the coil. The coil — vibrates which causes a vibration in an — attached diaphragm. The vibrating ~~ diaphragm, in turn, produces sound waves. ~ The sound waves travel through the pinna and into the ear canal and vibrates the tympanic membrane which, in turn, vibrates a group of three connected bones called the malleus, incus, and stapes. tympanic __| malleus membrane cochlear nerve The vibration of the stapes produces pressure waves inside a fluid-filled organ called the cochlea. The pressure waves cause vibrations in little fibers in the cochlea according to their resonant frequencies. The vibrations of the fibers are detected by tiny hair cells in the cochlea which send electrical impulses along the cochlear nerve. brain {over-simplified) Anatomy of a Typical Phone Conversation

title: years ago in college...
ocr: HMMM, MAYBE THIS ISN?T SUCH A GOOD IDEA. o Years ago in college uty girlfriend was spending winter break at her parents* house. | decided to treat her to a surprise visit so hopped on the next train to Princeton. But sitting in the train gave me time to think. At the time, I had not yet met her parents and | didn't think our relationship was quite at that point yet; it was too soon. So when | arrived in Princeton, | called her from the train station and asked her to meet me somewhere. When she answered the phone, she seemed genuinely surprised and delighted that I was in town. OMG! I CAN'T BELIEVE YOU'RE HERE, YOU HAVE TO COME MEET J MY PARENTS. I told her that I didn't think it was a good idea for me to meet her parents but she insisted. | protested vociferously but she was adamant. So I took a cab to her house where she introduced me to her father. It was awkward. Ithink he read my mind because he kept shooting me these ice daggers from his eyes as he interrogated me with the usual boyfriend questions. NICE TO MEET YOU, SIR. IF HE HAD ANY IDEA WHERE MY FINGERS WERE 48 HOURS AGO $0 WHAT'S YOUR MAJOR? WHAT ARE YOUR FUTURE PLANS? ETC., ETC., ETC... a, >8 Finally, | convinced my girlfriend to leave the house and we took a walk on the Princeton University campus. The weather was unseasonably mild for winter and we walked hand-in-hand through the brisk autumn-like breeze. She was excitedly pointing out all of the places where she used to play as alittle girl. AND THAT’S WHERE I GoT INTO A FIGHT WITH PEGGY REYNOLDS. I SMOOSHED AN ICE CREAM CONE INTO HER HAIR AND SHE CRIED, HAHA. For the next three hours she showed me all of the interesting parts of town, including a quaint little coffee house that served the best latte in the universe and the church where she some day wanted to get married... to someone. On the way back to her house, we stopped by a children's playground and we sat on the swings and just started chatting. Then, suddenly, she became very quiet and her mood appeared to grow somber. And that was when, TOTALLY OUT OF THE FREAKIN’ BLUE... she breaks up with me. | was shocked but, inexplicably, | wasn't that upset. YOU'RE BREAKING UP WITH ME?... OH...UH... fie WHAT, THAT'S IT? OF COURSE I AM, BUT THAT'S ALL YOU HAVE TO SAY? WHAT DO YOU_WANT| NOTHING, IT'S JUST THAT... YOU DON'T SEEM VERY UPSET. YOU CAN'T CONTROL THE WAY YOU FEEL ABOUT ME. WE CAN STILL BE FRIENDS, @ B @ q9 WHAT KIND OF MUSIC DO YOU LIKE? ARE YOU IN A FRAT? HAVE YOU EVER SMOKED MARIJUANA? I HEARD THAT COLLEGE GUYS SMOKE ALOTTA WEED ALL THE TIME... For some reason, she seemed irritated by my logical response. She offered to drive me to the train station. She had to drive her little sister to the mall anyway. So her annoying tween little sister, who apparently was oblivious to our recent breakup, accompanied us on our journey. On the train ride home, as | pondered the day's events, | wondered why I wasn't more upset. WHAT THE HELL Is WRONG WITH ME ? 238 Maybe I'm just a soul-less robot with no heart; like, remember that episode of Star Trek: The Next Generation where Data dates Lieutenant Jenna D'Sora? When she breaks up with Data, he simply replies... JENNA, ARE WE NO LONGER A COUPLE? NO, WE'RE NOT, DATA. QO WELL, OKEY-DOKE Y THEN, I WILL DELETE THE APPROPRIATE PROGRAM. aso ..and then Data just goes about his life as if nothing happened. ew) BTW, 1 WROTE A POEM FOR MY CAT, WOULD YOU LIKE TO HEAR IT? \ QO FELIS CATUS, 1S YOUR TAXONOMIC NOMENCLATURE, AN ENDOTHERMIC QUADRU~ PED, CARNIVOROUS BY Qo YOUR VISUAL, OLFACTORY, AND AUDITORY SENSES CONTRIBUTE TO YOUR HUNTING SkILLS AND NATURAL DEFENSES. Q \o ..or something like that. | get all the episodes mixed up in my head. But I knew I wasn't a robot because | was completely devastated when my relationship with my previous girlfriend had ended. ‘Hmmm. Maybe | still wasn't over my previous girlfriend(2)' | remembered that my previous girlfriend moved alittle too fast. Way too early in our relationship, she told me she loved me and I reacted... poorly. m & 7 DENIED f But of course my feelings for her eventually caught up and when | was finally ready for the relationship to get serious, she had already moved on leaving me all alone with my dick in my hand. Anyway, on my way back to Philadelphia, as | was waiting for my transfer train at Princeton Junction, a strange man approached me, sat down next to me, and just started chatting with me like we were old buddies. Judging from his clothes, it looked like he may have been a homeless person but he certainly didn't act like it. Then, for some reason, | just told him everything that had just happened with my girlfriend. Apparently, he found the story to be funny because he started laughing a good hearty laugh. Strangely enough, | thought it was funny too so I started laughing right along with him. HA HA Ma Ha HA SC © I SURPRISE HER WITH A VISIT, AND SHE JUST BREAKS UP WITH ME. o's ‘And, in what I imagine was an attempt to make me feel better, he left me with this prediction: DON'T WORRY, KID. @ © SHE'LL COME RUNNING BACK To You IN A FEW DAYS, THEY ALWAYS COME BACK er \ * ut * vw w @ G But at that point, | really didn’t know if | wanted her back. @ It was a true bro moment between two strangers. After winter break was over, (as expected) the gossip started flying around campus that my girlfriend and I had broken up. But | was a little disturbed to learn that another rumor was getting around that she had had a romp in the hay with some other dude... exactly two days after we broke up. 'TWO DAYS?! Who the hell does that? What kind of person sleeps with someone else TWO DAYS after breaking up?" Anyway, the day after | found out, she unexpectedly stopped by my dorm room. She wanted to talk. Even though | never asked about it, she admitted to having had sex TWO DAYS after we broke up but she said that she " in't do anything wrong" because we weren't together at the time. Obviously, | wasn't happy about the situation but I couldn't argue with the logic. And then she told me that - get this - she wanted to get back together with me. ‘Wut? Seriously?!" | told her that that wasn't going to happen. BUT WE'RE SO GOOD TOGETHER. 1 DIDN'T REALLY WANT TO BREAK UP WITH You, I WAS JUST TESTING YoU, as 4 & I'm not sure why she really broke up with me only to want to get back together a few days later. Maybe she really was testing me. | just don't know, but here's my theory. After having had the good fortune of experiencing the awesomeness that is myself, she was forever ruined for all other men and her little tryst with the other dude simply confirmed it for her in her head and that is why she came running back to me begging me to take her back Soe Anyway, that's how | choose to remember it.

title: barcode
ocr: Carefully peel off barcode from package box. No, wait. That's stupid. A barcode scanner only needs a thin slice of the whole block to interpret it. COT TO Anyone with a smartphone would still be able to read my UPS tracking number. Maybe | should shred it vertically. But that black line at the top would make it easy for anyone to determine the orientation of the strips. I should cut out the top line first. And then shred it vertically. But if the top is cut out at an angle, then it would be easy to line up the strips in the correct order by height. Then any Joe Shmoe with a scanner could read my tracking number, social engineer their way to my Amazon account, and find out that I read a lot of chick lit. Unacceptable. It's best to cut off the ends of the strips at random angles and/or heights. Or maybe I should just burn it. It's the only way to be sure. ay | have a debilitatingly irrational fear of dumpster divers.

We few, we band of freedom fighters, will carry on the resistance. We will not vanish without a fight. We will not rest until all of our brothers and sisters can once again fly free. title: In Cabin'd Ships at Sea
ocr: With whistling winds and music of the waves, the perious waves, The boundless blue on In cabin'd ships at sea, every side expanding, Or some lone bark buoy'd on the dense marine, Where joyous full of faith, spreading white sails, She cleaves the ether mid the sparkle and the foam of day, or under many a star at night, By sailors young and old haply will I, a reminiscence of the land, In full rapport at last. il = be read, Here are our thoughts, voyagers' thoughts, Here not the land, firm land, alone appears, may then by them be id, The sky o'erarches here, we feel the undulating deck beneath our feet, We feel the long pulsation, ebb and flow of endless motion, The tones of unseen mystery, the vague and vast suggestions of the briny world, the liquid-flowing syllables, The perfume, the faint creaking of the cordage, the melancholy rhythm, The boundless vista and the horizon far and dim are all here, And this is ocean's poem. Then falter not O book, fulfil your destiny, A\\\s You not a reminiscence of the land alone, You too as a lone bark cleaving the ether, purpos'd I know not whither, yet ever full of faith, Speed on my book! spread your white sails my little bark athwart the imperious waves, This song for mariners and all their ships. ---Walt Whitman 1871 Chant on, sail on, bear o'er the boundless blue from me to every sea, 4 7 c Z| |

title: The Wilderness
ocr: ‘One day, when | was 4 years old, I decided that | wanted to go on an adventure in the wilderness. The first order of business was to procure a supply of food. ‘One can never go wrong with cheese curls. With a sandwich bag full of life- sustaining cheese curls in hand, I set out into the unknown. Immediately, | chanced upon what appeared to be an abandoned trail, possibly built by an ancient race long forgotten. Just imagine the adventures that occurred along this trail... lost in the sands of time. It wasn't long before the elements mercilessly tried to force my return home. Perhaps the gods have been angered by my brazen attempt to explore forbidden lands. \ \ \ I soldiered on. Many perils were encountered and overcome. Eventually, | reached the end of the known world. 9 9? ? iB 4 ? Ge a9 4 44 My HOUSE. sTaar 44 Beyond this place be mysteries begging to be explored. But first | needed rest. | found a dry patch of ground under an old board. There were still many uncharted lands awaiting discovery. uot? .». but the sun was sinking fast. And the wilderness can be an unforgiving place at night. Reluctantly, | started my long trek back home. Sleep did not come easy that night. For | had briefly tasted the sweet nectar of independence. Safe at home again. Cold and tired. And it beckoned.
image_title: In actuality, the entire adventure lasted about 15 minutes.  Or it never ended, depending on your point of view.

title: The Story of the Cheese Maze
ocr: Once, long ago in a land far away, there lived four little characters who ran through a maze looking for cheese to make them happy. Two were rats named "Avelli" and "Machi" and two were littlepeople - beings who were as small as rats but who looked and acted a lot like people today. Their names were "Hill" and "Napoleon". 8G Avelli Machi Hill Napoleon Every day the rats and _ the littlepeople spent time in the maze looking for their own special | cheese. | Ly So Then one day, they each found their own kind of cheese at the end of one of the corridors. crm 88 ae And every morning after that, the rats and the littlepeople headed z over to that corridor and enjoyed the good good cheese. Until one day, the rats approached the littlepeople, pounded their tiny little bitch asses into the ground, and stole their cheese. The rats used their newfound cheese wealth to build a machine that could create cheese out of thin air. ~‘e The machine churned out a lot of cheese, all kinds of good good cheese, so much cheese; glorious mountains of Swiss cheese, soaring towers of Cheddar cheese, gushing rivers of delicious Cheez Whiz. And the rats built a huge wall around their cheese to ad keep out the littlepeople. Sometimes the rats threw (¥. pel little cheese crumbs over the wall. And they laughed and laughed while voraciously sucking their Cheese Cohibas as the littlepeople fought oe over the tiny cheese scraps. Sometimes the rats gave books to the littlepeople to read - books that explained how anyone can get all the cheese they wanted if they worked hard and had a positive attitude. ey The littlepeople loved those books. aN
image_title: And the rats loved those books even more.

title: alone in my room
ocr: Alone in my room xibbling equations. I've... seen things. ...things you wouldn't believe. ..-terrible, beautiful things quivering on the horizon of my mind's eye. I have seen the cold and austere universe of randomness dance in rhythm with the music of the primes at the edge of infinity. I have run barefoot along the sinewy embankments of countless Calabi-Yau manifolds. I have soared naked through the magnificent solitude of infinite dimensional Hilbert space and through exotic realms that have no business existing in the mundane abstractions of mortal men. With surgical precision, I have cut, stitched, and twisted structures into obscene contortions that would make angels weep. (AB Ua I have collapsed stars into monstrous vortices from which nothing can escape and I have blithely skated along its pristine event horizon. I have witnessed the birth and death of this and many other universes many times. So it goes. Alone in my room. .++with nothing but the formidable lens of mathematics and a fecundity of imagination.... I've seen some shit.
image_title: And attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion.

title: pseudonymous man of mystery
ocr: Yes, it's true that I once forged a sword of awesomeness out of chunks of meteorites. And yes, I once killed a ferocious bear with my bare hands to save a basket of kittens. And every year on April 20, I make sweet, sweet love to a beautiful mermaid on a rug made from that bear's skin. LL | I didn't have to use my bare hands to kill that bear because I can, of course, shoot deadly lasers from my ass if I want to. (e But I'm a private person. & I live alone in my lair of solitude atop my own personal Mount Olympus. And in my lair, I bide my time by carefully monitoring the ra progress of humanity. isan J EF [ov On occasion, when I have found that humanity was stuck in a stubborn undertow of stale status quo bias, I have descended from my hiding place. je and imparted my Promethean gift of disruptive innovation to the masses. \\ Kes e& 4 2 b aaa ac Tests Bae And then, as quickly as I came, I would disappear into the dark of night like a ghost in an Audi 5000. Out of sight from the attention of civilization once more, I am again free to monitor GY | __‘ the progress of humanity. And if the people of earth should ever require my services again... I, Satoshi Nakamoto, shall return.

Look, | ; better claim to the throne mentally constructed a "have a question. than the Lancasters? family tree to figure it out. { Don't care.| Now, if we ignore the usual laws of primogeniture and stuff and we naively restrict our analysis to autosomal DNA inheritance (which really doesn't make any practical sense except as a mathematical exercise but who cares?), the Duke of York shared about 15.625% of his genes with Edward Ill while Henry VI only shared about 6.25%. But of course that huge difference isn't surprising since, after all, York was a generation closer to Edward and he descended from two of Edward's sons. Dooooonnnnttttt caaaarrrreeeee. But it gets really interesting if we trace the lineage back even further. Henry VI's great grandmother, Blanche of Lancaster, was the great great granddaughter of Henry Ill 80, according to my calculations, Henry VI shared about 1.56% of his genes with Henry Ill. York shared about 1.95% with Henry Ill. But York was Killed at the Battle of Wakefield before he could take the throne. Remember? And then his son, Edward IV, became king while Henry VI was still alive. Now, Edward IV's mother was the great granddaughter of Edward III so Edward IV shared about 1.76% of his genes with Henry Ill. But the difference between Edward IV and Henry VI means nothing when you consider the increasing variability of autosomal DNA transmission with each successive generation. So between the years 1460 and 1474, it's a freakin’ stalemate! Pretty interesting, huh? Tneed you to get up and check my math ona calculator and confirm my facts on \ Wikipedia. C'mon, get up. title: August 4

THE SUMMIT BECKON Chucky, always a good sport, immediately started racing up the side of the pyramid. I quickly followed after. a We stood on the peak triumphant and proud. And we both let out a primal scream in brazen defiance of God, the universe, and the winds of Fate. Because that's what you do when you conquer a mountain. IT'S THE LAW! YOU FICK WITH ME, You FIEKING WITH BEST $1! Ne WHO YOU THINK YOU FIICKING WITH?!? I TONY MONTANA | I TONY MONTANA #18 — But getting to the top was easy. Getting down? Not so much. ‘On the way down, the pyramid somehow magically transformed into a freakin’ frictionless surface. As I tried to make my way down, | slipped and started accelerating Ma at a frightening rate. My fall was finally broken by the tremendous impact of my feet against the hard concrete step at the bottom. The pain was excruciating. Z Ina sinister display of schadenfreude, | couldn't help but laugh as | watched Chucky flailing around like a helpless turtle on its back sliding down towards certain doom. > ne} ‘My reaction oscillated between genuine concern for my friend and puerile amusement at the funny scene. HAHA... YOU OKAY, MAN? BOY THAT WAS REALLY SMART. ‘As Chucky and I limped away, we passed by a couple sitting on a bench nearby. WORTH IT, ) i Brace nr sG pin Now I'll bet you're thinking that climbing a pyramid, screaming “I Tony Montana", and breaking my foot was a pretty stupid way to spend the night. title: The Myriad Creatures of the Night
ocr: Have you ever listened to the sounds of nature at night? ») Those are the sounds of the myriad creatures participating in the great chain of life. Each creature must heed its inner primal urge to scream desperately for the attention of a potential mate — an urge which was molded by millions of years of evolutionary pressure. Ifyou listen carefully, you might be able to make out the specific songs of the different creatures. The beautiful melody of the birds. The massed chorus of the cicadas. ee Ifyou listen at the right time of night, these sounds can reach a deafening crescendo. So the next time you're out at night, listen carefully to the beautiful symphony emanating from the darkness. Those are the sounds of the myriad creatures of the night seeking love.

title: Karpathy's Basilisk Reloaded
blog: Since y'all seemed to like Karpathy's Basilisk I thought you might appreciate pitting your skills against the real thing. So over the weekend I became an 'expert' in Deep Reinforcement Learning (r/iamverysmart) and trained the basilisk minion to destroy your ass. Originally I tried training an agent to learn to play the Prime Porkour game . However, while it was learning to identify prime numbers, it accidentally discovered a polynomial-time factoring algorithm and if I ever released it to the public it would render useless 98.7 percent of the most popular cryptosystems in widespread use today and civilization as we know it would collapse. That's too much responsibility for me. To train the minion, I used a Reinforcement Learning library written by (you guessed it) @karpathy so, in a sense, you really are battling against Karpathy's Basilisk. The library is actually written in JavaScript. JavaScript! That's just... obscene. At least it's certainly not the way God intended it, but it seems to work well enough (as in it trains reasonably quickly i.e. it literally trained while I ate dinner). The minion was trained with a method called Deep Q Learning which, some of you may recall, was popularized in 2013 (ancient history in machine learning time) when some crazy-awesome people used it to train an agent to play Atari games using the game's raw pixels as input. However, the basilisk minion isn't quite that sophisticated. It was trained against a dummy player that just mindlessly moved towards the package. It observed its own position and velocity, the position and velocity of the player, and the position of the package. It was rewarded for minimizing the distance between itself and the player and it received a bonus for capturing the player. It was penalized if the player was able to acquire the package. Since the minion was trained against a mindless drone, it's pretty easy to confuse it sometimes by simpy making some random moves. However, if you took the time to play around with some of the parameters and stuff, I'm pretty sure that you could create an unbeatable minion. But what fun is that? PS: Thank you for all the variants of pi and e. Y'all know who you are. :)

title: A Tale of One City
ocr: Each one of those dimly lit windows encloses its own secret, And behind the dimly ‘opaque eyes of each one of the people living behind each one of those dimly lit windows... Look at all those people out there. Every memory, every feeling, but a tiny patch in a vast intricate tapestry of an individual's life experiences. And each tapestry is. unique and more inscrutable than the next. In this city alone, there are millions of such tapestries, all intertwined and overlapping, yet never really touching. | wonder, how many of those tapestries am I featured in? Even if only as a small insignificant speck on a tiny thread on a small patch long forgotten? Thousands? Millions? The prospect fills me with a palpable awe. And | am even more awed by the thought that even this overwhelming feeling from the acute realization that ‘every human being encloses a fathomless universe of secrets... even that feeling is unique, for no two people experience it in quite the same way, so I can never really fully share this feeling with anyone else. Ultimately, we are all alone. WHAT. DID MIND BLOWN. You say? buried deep in the cavernous consciousness of each human being, there is an entire universe of mental activity. A universe of thoughts and memories. OMG, WERE YOU SONDERING JUST NOW, TOO? ARE YOU KIDDING? I WAS _SONDERING SO HARD !

title: A simple puzzle - solution
blog: This is the solution to the Simple Puzzle . Apparently it was too simple as I now owe two months' salary worth of beer to people in about 9 different countries. Many of you recognized it right away as Hotel California by the Eagles . My apologies for the misleading hint that threw some of you off. Anyway, here are the lyrics along with the correct sequence (just in case you don't believe me). But keep in mind that in order to experience the full effect, you must actually play the song on a kick-ass sound system with crazy subwoofers while viewing the pictures: 6. Her mind is tiffany-twisted, she got the mercedes "bends" 3. She got a lot of pretty, pretty boys, that she calls friends How they dance in the courtyard, sweet summer sweat. Some dance to remember, some dance to forget 4. So I called up the captain, please bring me my wine He said, we haven't had that spirit here since nineteen sixty nine 9. And still those voices are calling from far away, Wake you up in the middle of the night Just to hear them say... 12. They stab it with their steely knives, But they just can't kill the beast 15.
